title: Oman's Dhamani Platform attains 60% pharmacy network linkage | HealthCare Middle East & Africa - No.1 Health & Pharma Manufacturing Industry Publication
summary: The platform now enables the secure exchange of approvals, billing, and settlements among pharmacies, insurers, and healthcare providers, reducing reliance on manual paperwork by up to 98% in cert ...
thbAction: Position THB's pharma CRM and omnichannel HCP engagement workflows to support launch planning, KAM orchestration, and compliant follow-up across India and the Middle East.
contentHtml:
OMAN—Oman has advanced its national digital health transformation as the Financial Services Authority (FSA) continues to scale up the Dhamani health insurance platform, which connects healthcare providers, pharmacies, and insurers through a unified digital network.

The latest expansion introduces pharmacies into Phase II of the platform, enabling fully electronic prescription (e-Rx) sharing across the healthcare ecosystem.

This development allows prescriptions issued in private healthcare facilities to be transmitted directly to pharmacies in real time.

As a result, patients can access medication faster, while healthcare providers and insurers coordinate more efficiently through a single digital system.

Building a fully digital prescription journey

With the Phase II rollout, Dhamani now supports an end-to-end digital prescription process that eliminates paper-based documentation.

Doctors issue prescriptions electronically, which are then routed to pharmacies for dispensing and later processed for insurance claims and settlements within the same platform.

According to the FSA, this integrated workflow significantly reduces administrative delays and improves coordination between stakeholders.

It also strengthens transparency in claims processing and helps standardize pharmacy-related transactions across the insurance system.

The platform now enables the secure exchange of approvals, billing, and settlements among pharmacies, insurers, and healthcare providers, reducing reliance on manual paperwork by up to 98% in certain processes.

Rapid adoption among pharmacies

The expansion has already recorded notable uptake, with around 271 pharmacies registered on the platform and connectivity reaching approximately 60% of the pharmacy network.

This growth reflects increasing adoption of digital tools within Oman’s healthcare sector.

Pharmacies can now receive prescriptions directly without physical documents, allowing them to serve patients more quickly and efficiently while reducing operational bottlenecks.

Strengthening Oman’s digital health ecosystem

Recent coverage highlights that Dhamani has become one of the key pillars of Oman’s broader health digitalization strategy.

Since its initial rollout, the platform has processed millions of insurance transactions and claims, demonstrating its capacity to support large-scale national healthcare operations.

The system continues to expand its integration scope, linking hospitals, clinics, insurers, and now pharmacies into a single interoperable framework.

This growing connectivity is positioning Oman as a regional leader in digital health insurance transformation.

Enhancing efficiency and reducing paper-based systems

The FSA notes that the integrated system reduces administrative workload across the sector by streamlining approvals, claims processing, and reimbursement cycles.

The digital model minimizes paperwork, improves accuracy in financial transactions, and shortens the time required for pharmacies and insurers to complete settlements.

By digitizing the entire prescription-to-claim journey, the platform also supports better regulatory oversight and improves data consistency across healthcare institutions.

Expanding national health insurance digitization

The Dhamani platform continues to evolve as part of Oman’s broader strategy to modernize healthcare delivery.

Ongoing enhancements focus on improving interoperability between healthcare providers and insurers while expanding digital services that simplify patient access to care.

The FSA has indicated that further onboarding of pharmacies and healthcare providers will continue as part of Phase II implementation, reinforcing the country’s push toward a fully integrated digital health insurance ecosystem.
